name: Tuna Vegetable Chowder
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, melt margarine over medium heat. Cook onion, garlic, carrots, and celery, stirring occasionally, for 5 to 8 minutes or until softened.
2. Add potatoes, chicken stock, dill, and lemon rind. Bring mixture to a boil.
3. Reduce heat to medium-low. Cover and simmer for 12 to 15 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
4. Drain tuna, reserving liquid. Break tuna into large chunks and add them to the pan along with the reserved liquid, milk, corn, peas, salt, and pepper.
5. Heat through, adjusting seasoning as needed.
6. Makes 8 cups or 4 to 6 servings.
Per Serving (6) - Calories 154.1, Total Fat 1.5, 8.6% of CFF
NOTES: 2% milk can be substituted for skim milk, but the calorie and fat content will increase. My husband prefers I use 2% instead of skim milk, but he likes it either way.
